The error is clear — your Render deployment is crashing because `SESSION_SECRET` (or `INBOX_ENC_KEY`) isn't set as an environment variable on Render. The app enforces this in production. Let me check the exact code to confirm and see if there are any other required vars.

This is a straightforward fix — your Render deployment is missing required environment variables. The app intentionally crashes in production if `SESSION_SECRET` isn't set (to prevent running with no security).

**What you need to do on Render:**

Go to your service on [render.com](https://render.com) → **Environment** → add these variables:

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `SESSION_SECRET` | Any long random string (e.g. 64 random characters) |
| `MONGODB_URI` | Your MongoDB Atlas connection string |
| `KIMI_API_KEY` | Your Moonshot/Kimi API key |

Then add any others from `replit.md` that your features use (payment keys, shipping keys, etc.) — they won't crash the app if missing, but those features won't work.

You can generate a strong `SESSION_SECRET` with this command in your terminal:
```bash
node -e "console.log(require('crypto').randomBytes(64).toString('hex'))"
```

Once you add `SESSION_SECRET` and `MONGODB_URI` and redeploy, the startup crash will be gone.

**Next, I can add a graceful startup check that logs all missing env vars at once (instead of crashing on the first one), so you can see everything needed in a single deploy attempt. Would you like me to do that?**
